channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: European Solidarity Corps Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026 in Hungary: Paid Facilitator and Rapporteur Opportunities Open for International Experts
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Professionals with expertise in facilitation, reporting, youth programmes, accreditation systems, and international learning environments now have an exciting opportunity to join a major European-level event focused on improving the quality and effectiveness of the European Solidarity Corps Quality Label process. The National Agency Tempus Public Foundation in Hungary, in cooperation with SALTO European Solidarity Corps, […]

The post European Solidarity Corps Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026 in Hungary: Paid Facilitator and Rapporteur Opportunities Open for International Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals with expertise in facilitation, reporting, youth programmes, accreditation systems, and international learning environments now have an exciting opportunity to join a major European-level event focused on improving the quality and effectiveness of the European Solidarity Corps Quality Label process.

The National Agency Tempus Public Foundation in Hungary, in cooperation with SALTO European Solidarity Corps, has officially launched a call for:

  • 2 Facilitators
  • 2 Rapporteurs

for the International European Solidarity Corps Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026.

The meeting will bring together experts, representatives of National Agencies, SALTO Resource Centres, and Quality Label professionals from programme and partner countries to strengthen collaboration, improve accreditation processes, and exchange experiences related to the Quality Label framework within the European Solidarity Corps Programme.

This international meeting represents a valuable professional opportunity for experienced facilitators and rapporteurs interested in:

  • European youth programmes
  • Accreditation systems
  • International cooperation
  • Non-formal learning
  • Policy discussions
  • Professional networking
  • Community building
  • Programme quality improvement

The selected professionals will receive fully covered travel, accommodation, and board expenses, alongside professional fees for their work.

About the International Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026

The “International Quality Label Experts Meeting” is a 3.5-working-day international event designed to improve the Quality Label process within the European Solidarity Corps Programme.

The event will begin with an informal evening programme and continue with intensive working sessions focused on:

  • Quality Label accreditation procedures
  • Assessment and monitoring processes
  • Sharing best practices
  • Peer learning
  • Community building
  • Professional reflection
  • Future recommendations for accreditation systems

The meeting is organised by:

  • The National Agency Tempus Public Foundation, Hungary
  • SALTO European Solidarity Corps Resource Centre
  • International Quality Label Working Group

The event aims to strengthen both the Quality Label system and the professional community supporting it.

Objectives of the Meeting

The overall objective of the meeting is to improve:

  • The quality of the existing Quality Label process
  • The work and effectiveness of Quality Label experts

Participants and professionals involved in the event will engage in activities that support:

  1. Experience sharing among Quality Label experts
  2. Peer learning and knowledge exchange
  3. Networking and community culture development
  4. Understanding of European Solidarity Corps procedures
  5. Reflection on the role of Quality Label experts
  6. Discussions on current challenges affecting accreditation systems
  7. Development of recommendations for the future Erasmus+ Programme and Quality Label procedures

This international gathering is expected to foster stronger collaboration among professionals working within European youth and volunteering programmes.

Who Will Attend the Meeting?

The meeting is addressed to:

  • Representatives of National Agencies
  • SALTO Resource Centres
  • External Quality Label experts
  • Internal Quality Label experts
  • Experts from programme countries
  • Experts from partner countries

Participants may have varying levels of experience, creating a rich environment for exchange, collaboration, and professional development.

Dates and Venue

Meeting Dates

16–20 November 2026

Schedule:

  • 16 November: Informal welcome evening
  • 17–19 November: Full working days
  • 20 November: Departure day

Venue

The meeting will be held residentially in Hungary.

Working Language

English

Paid Facilitator Opportunity

The organisers are seeking 2 experienced facilitators to support the design, implementation, and evaluation of the international meeting.

Facilitators will play a critical role in:

  • Leading sessions
  • Supporting discussions
  • Facilitating group processes
  • Encouraging participation
  • Managing learning dynamics
  • Supporting collaborative reflection

Facilitator Eligibility and Required Profile

Applicants for the facilitator role must demonstrate:

Professional Experience

  • Strong experience facilitating international seminars, conferences, or training activities
  • Experience working with large groups of approximately 50–80 participants
  • Knowledge of Quality Label procedures and accreditation systems
  • Understanding of the European Solidarity Corps Programme

Skills and Competencies

  • Ability to facilitate peer learning and knowledge exchange
  • Community-building and networking skills
  • Strong communication and moderation skills
  • Flexibility in adapting programmes to participants’ needs
  • Ability to work in multicultural and international teams
  • Excellent spoken and written English
  • Digital facilitation competencies

Additional Advantage

  • Experience with online/open agenda tools

Important Requirement

Applicants must hold a valid EU VAT number for contractual purposes.

Facilitator Responsibilities

Selected facilitators will:

  • Participate in preparatory meetings
  • Design the methodological flow of the meeting
  • Facilitate plenary sessions and group activities
  • Support participant engagement
  • Adapt methodologies during the event
  • Lead evaluations
  • Support reporting and documentation processes

Facilitator Fee and Working Conditions

Daily Fee

340 EUR per day

Working Days Calculation

  • 3.5 days preparation
  • 3.5 meeting working days
  • 2 evaluation and follow-up days

Total

9 working days

Total Compensation

3,060 EUR

In addition:

  • Accommodation is fully covered
  • Meals are fully covered
  • Travel expenses are fully covered

Paid Rapporteur Opportunity

The organisers are also recruiting 2 rapporteurs responsible for documenting and reporting the meeting outcomes.

Rapporteurs will help ensure that discussions, recommendations, and key insights are accurately captured and presented in structured reports.

Rapporteur Eligibility and Required Profile

Candidates applying as rapporteurs should demonstrate:

Experience

  • Proven experience reporting at international meetings or conferences
  • Understanding of EU youth programmes and terminology
  • Experience with digital reporting systems

Skills

  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • Strong organisational skills
  • Ability to synthesise complex information
  • Digital content management skills
  • Visualisation skills using platforms like:
    • SharePoint
    • MS Teams
    • Canva

Additional Requirements

  • Experience with conference management tools
  • Valid EU VAT number

Rapporteur Responsibilities

Selected rapporteurs will:

  • Participate in preparatory meetings
  • Create digital reporting templates
  • Produce daily summaries
  • Support workshop documentation
  • Capture key meeting outcomes
  • Take event photographs
  • Draft executive summaries and final reports
  • Submit final reports within four weeks after the event

Rapporteur Fee and Working Conditions

Daily Fee

340 EUR per day

Work Duration

  • 2 preparation days
  • 3 event days
  • 2 reporting days

Total

7 working days

Total Compensation

2,380 EUR

Additionally:

  • Travel expenses are covered
  • Accommodation is covered
  • Meals and lodging are covered

Why This Opportunity Is Valuable

This opportunity offers much more than financial compensation. Selected professionals will:

  • Gain visibility within European youth programme networks
  • Collaborate with international experts
  • Contribute to improving European accreditation systems
  • Build long-term professional relationships
  • Strengthen facilitation or reporting portfolios
  • Participate in high-level European discussions

For trainers, facilitators, rapporteurs, youth workers, programme coordinators, and EU project professionals, this can become an important career development opportunity.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early before the deadline.

Selection Notification Date

Selected applicants will be informed about the outcome by: 15 June 2026

How to Apply

Facilitator Application Link

Facilitator Application Form

For more information click here

Rapporteur Application Link

Rapporteur Application Form

For more information click here

 Final Thoughts

The International European Solidarity Corps Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026 presents a remarkable opportunity for experienced facilitators and rapporteurs to contribute to the future of European youth programmes, accreditation systems, and quality assurance processes.

With strong professional compensation, international networking opportunities, and involvement in high-level European discussions, the opportunity is ideal for professionals passionate about:

  • Youth development
  • European cooperation
  • Accreditation systems
  • Facilitation
  • International learning
  • Community building
  • Programme quality improvement

Professionals with relevant expertise and experience are encouraged to apply before the deadline and become part of this impactful international initiative in Hungary.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post European Solidarity Corps Quality Label Experts Meeting 2026 in Hungary: Paid Facilitator and Rapporteur Opportunities Open for International Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Indigenous and Pacific Leadership Program
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity (AFSE) Fellowship 2027, a prestigious Indigenous and Pacific-centred fellowship programme designed to empower changemakers, strengthen Indigenous-led initiatives, and advance social equity across Australia, Aotearoa New Zealand, and Pacific Island nations. Hosted at the University of Melbourne and supported by Waipapa Taumata Rau University […]

The post Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Indigenous and Pacific Leadership Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity (AFSE) Fellowship 2027, a prestigious Indigenous and Pacific-centred fellowship programme designed to empower changemakers, strengthen Indigenous-led initiatives, and advance social equity across Australia, Aotearoa New Zealand, and Pacific Island nations.

Hosted at the University of Melbourne and supported by Waipapa Taumata Rau University of Auckland, the fellowship is one of the globally recognised Atlantic Fellows programmes established through the philanthropic vision of the late Charles “Chuck” F. Feeney.

The AFSE Fellowship is specifically designed for emerging and established Indigenous and Pacific leaders, as well as allies committed to Indigenous-led social transformation. Through academic learning, leadership development, community engagement, and collaborative networking, Fellows are equipped to drive long-term systems change grounded in Indigenous and Pacific knowledges, practices, and worldviews.

The programme combines:

  • A fee-free University of Melbourne Master’s degree or Graduate Certificate
  • Intensive leadership and social change training
  • Community-based learning
  • International networking opportunities
  • Ongoing lifelong fellowship support
  • Access to a global network of changemakers

This transformative fellowship supports individuals already working within communities, organisations, governments, businesses, healthcare systems, the arts, and advocacy spaces who are committed to creating a more just and equitable world.

What Is the Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity (AFSE)?

The Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity is a transformative fellowship programme focused on advancing Indigenous-led social change and strengthening Indigenous and Pacific leadership.

The programme operates on a powerful principle:

Indigenous and Pacific Peoples already hold deep knowledge, wisdom, and solutions capable of addressing community challenges and contributing to global transformation.

The fellowship therefore aims to:

  • Centre Indigenous and Pacific knowledge systems
  • Support social equity initiatives
  • Empower community-focused leaders
  • Build stronger Indigenous-led systems change movements
  • Create collaborative leadership networks

AFSE brings together Fellows from diverse sectors and experiences while fostering deep learning, mutual support, and long-term collaboration.

Host Institutions

The fellowship is hosted and supported by leading institutions including:

  • University of Melbourne
  • Waipapa Taumata Rau University of Auckland
  • Atlantic Institute
  • Rhodes Trust

The Atlantic Institute, hosted at Rhodes House in Oxford, United Kingdom, serves as the global convening and knowledge-sharing hub for Atlantic Fellows worldwide.

A Global Fellowship Community

AFSE is one of seven Atlantic Fellows programmes operating globally.

After completing the Foundation Year, Fellows become part of:

  • A lifelong Atlantic Fellows network
  • A community of over 1,100 changemakers
  • Fellows across more than 70 countries

This global network creates opportunities for:

  • International collaboration
  • Cross-cultural learning
  • Social innovation
  • Global summits
  • Shared leadership initiatives

Senior Fellows continue to receive support, learning opportunities, and collaborative engagement long after the completion of the programme.

The Fellowship Journey

The Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship consists of two interconnected phases:

1. Foundation Year

During the Foundation Year:

  • Up to 20 Indigenous and Pacific leaders and allies are selected
  • Fellows engage in collaborative learning
  • Participants develop long-lasting professional and personal relationships
  • Fellows complete a fee-free academic qualification

The academic component includes:

  • A Master’s degree in Social Change Leadership
    OR
  • A Graduate Certificate in Social Change Leadership

The programme integrates:

  • Indigenous and Pacific knowledges
  • Critical thinking
  • Systems change approaches
  • Community-based learning
  • Experiential development
  • Academic scholarship

The Foundation Year is intentionally designed to allow Fellows to remain closely connected to their communities while studying.

2. Lifelong Fellowship

Upon completing the Foundation Year, Fellows transition into the lifelong fellowship community as Senior Fellows.

This stage includes:

  • Continued collaboration opportunities
  • Global networking
  • Social change support
  • Participation in Atlantic Fellows summits
  • Access to international changemaker communities

The lifelong fellowship strengthens ongoing impact and supports Fellows in expanding their initiatives and leadership work.

Social Change Initiative

One of the central components of the fellowship is the Social Change Initiative.

Each Fellow develops and advances a practical project or body of work focused on addressing real-world community issues.

These initiatives may include:

  • Public policy advocacy
  • Community-led programmes
  • Health equity initiatives
  • Climate justice solutions
  • Storytelling and media projects
  • Educational transformation
  • Cultural preservation
  • Systems reform efforts

The initiative is designed to:

  • Translate knowledge into action
  • Create long-term community impact
  • Strengthen Indigenous-led solutions
  • Advance social equity

Throughout the programme, Fellows receive support in refining and implementing these initiatives.

Programme Delivery and Learning Structure

The programme is delivered over one year and combines:

  • In-person learning
  • Online tutorials
  • Community engagement
  • Academic assessments
  • Collaborative learning

Unlike many residential fellowships, AFSE allows Fellows to spend most of the year within their home communities.

In-Person Learning Modules

The Foundation Year includes:

  • Five intensive in-person modules
  • 5–8 days per module
  • Approximately 40 days of travel during the year

The modules are held across:

  • Australia
  • Aotearoa New Zealand
  • Pacific Island nations

Some sessions are hosted directly within Indigenous and Pacific communities to deepen cultural learning and exchange.

Online Tutorials and Academic Engagement

Online tutorials take place every second week and involve:

  • Group discussions
  • Oral assessments
  • Collaborative reflection
  • Interactive learning

Assessment formats throughout the programme may include:

  • Written assignments
  • Oral presentations
  • Group projects
  • Reflective exercises
  • Applied project work

Fellows receive:

  • Academic feedback
  • Peer support
  • Leadership development guidance

Fully Funded Fellowship Benefits

The AFSE Fellowship provides substantial support to selected Fellows.

Academic Benefits

  • Fee-free Master’s degree or Graduate Certificate
  • Access to University of Melbourne academic resources
  • Indigenous and Pacific-centred curriculum

Travel and Accommodation Support

AFSE fully covers:

  • Travel costs
  • Accommodation
  • Programme-related expenses for in-person sessions

Leadership and Networking Opportunities

Fellows gain:

  • Access to international changemaker networks
  • Long-term collaboration opportunities
  • Leadership development
  • Mentorship and peer learning

Global Atlantic Fellows Network

Participants become part of:

  • A global fellowship network
  • International summits and convenings
  • Lifelong professional support structures

Who Should Apply?

The programme seeks:

  • Mid-career professionals
  • Community-focused leaders
  • Indigenous and Pacific changemakers
  • Allies committed to Indigenous-led equity work

Applicants may come from sectors including:

  • Community organisations
  • Government
  • Healthcare
  • Education
  • Business
  • Media
  • Arts and culture
  • Public policy
  • Advocacy
  • Environmental work

The fellowship is especially suitable for leaders already working on initiatives aimed at improving social equity and community wellbeing.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship is more than an academic programme. It is a transformative leadership experience grounded in:

  • Indigenous wisdom
  • Community-centred leadership
  • Systems change
  • Social justice
  • Equity-driven action

The fellowship creates space for Indigenous and Pacific leaders to:

  • Strengthen their impact
  • Expand their networks
  • Develop sustainable initiatives
  • Build collective power
  • Influence future systems and policies

2027 Fellowship Updates

AFSE has announced that important changes are planned for the 2027 Foundation Year experience to further strengthen:

  • Indigenous and Pacific on-country learning
  • Community exchange
  • Fellowship collaboration

Additional programme information is expected to be released in mid-2026.

Application Information

Applications for the 2027 Fellowship are now open.

Prospective applicants are encouraged to:

  • Review programme details carefully
  • Reflect on their social change initiative
  • Prepare for a collaborative and intensive fellowship experience

Apply Here

Deadline:

27 July 2026

Final Thoughts

The Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship 2027 represents a remarkable opportunity for Indigenous and Pacific leaders and allies committed to social transformation, systems change, and community empowerment.

By combining academic excellence, Indigenous-centred leadership, community engagement, and global collaboration, the fellowship creates a powerful platform for changemakers seeking to drive meaningful impact within their communities and beyond.

For individuals passionate about Indigenous knowledge systems, social equity, and sustainable community-led change, the AFSE Fellowship offers both the resources and the global network needed to strengthen leadership and create lasting transformation.

For programme details and application information, visit the official website.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Atlantic Fellows for Social Equity Fellowship 2027 at the University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Indigenous and Pacific Leadership Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland | €25,000 Annual Stipend for International and EU Students
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the highly prestigious School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 at University College Cork (UCC) in Ireland. This fully funded doctoral scholarship offers outstanding students the opportunity to pursue advanced legal research at one of Ireland’s leading universities while receiving comprehensive financial support. The scholarship is open to both EU […]

The post Fully Funded UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland | €25,000 Annual Stipend for International and EU Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the highly prestigious School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 at University College Cork (UCC) in Ireland. This fully funded doctoral scholarship offers outstanding students the opportunity to pursue advanced legal research at one of Ireland’s leading universities while receiving comprehensive financial support.

The scholarship is open to both EU and non-EU applicants and provides:

  • Full tuition fee coverage
  • An annual stipend of EUR 25,000
  • Funding support for up to four years

This opportunity is designed for ambitious scholars who are passionate about legal research, academic excellence, and contributing meaningful knowledge to the field of law.

With access to internationally recognised supervisors, a vibrant doctoral research community, and tailored academic support systems, the UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship represents an exceptional pathway for aspiring legal researchers and academics.

About University College Cork (UCC)

University College Cork (UCC) is one of Ireland’s leading higher education institutions, internationally recognised for academic excellence, research innovation, and student development.

The School of Law at UCC is known for:

  • High-quality legal research
  • Internationally respected faculty
  • Strong doctoral training
  • Interdisciplinary legal scholarship
  • Supportive academic environment

The university offers doctoral researchers access to:

  • Expert supervision
  • Research training
  • Academic resources
  • Networking opportunities
  • Professional development activities

Students joining the programme become part of a thriving research community committed to advancing legal scholarship and public impact.

Overview of the UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026

The School of Law PhD Scholarship is offered to candidates commencing PhD studies in Autumn 2026.

The scholarship supports students pursuing doctoral research in any field of law and aims to attract highly talented researchers with strong academic backgrounds and research potential.

This fully funded scholarship allows recipients to focus on high-level research without the burden of tuition costs or financial constraints.

Scholarship Benefits

The UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 provides substantial financial and academic support to successful candidates.

1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

The scholarship fully covers tuition fees for:

  • EU applicants
  • Non-EU applicants

This makes the programme accessible to international students from around the world.

2. Annual Stipend of EUR 25,000

Recipients will receive:

  • EUR 25,000 per year
  • Funding support for a maximum of four years

The stipend is intended to help cover living expenses and allow students to focus on their research activities.

3. Access to Internationally Recognised Supervisors

PhD scholars at UCC benefit from supervision by experts who are internationally recognised in their respective legal fields.

This provides students with:

  • High-level academic mentorship
  • Research guidance
  • Professional development support
  • Opportunities for scholarly collaboration

4. Vibrant Doctoral Research Community

Students become part of a dynamic and supportive doctoral environment with access to:

  • Research seminars
  • Workshops
  • Academic conferences
  • Collaborative research opportunities
  • Tailored doctoral support systems

5. Potential Teaching and Tutoring Opportunities

The university notes that separately remunerated opportunities in:

  • Lecturing
  • Tutoring

may also become available during the PhD programme.

These experiences can help doctoral students build teaching portfolios and strengthen future academic career prospects.

Research Areas

One of the major strengths of this scholarship is its flexibility regarding research topics.

The scholarship is open to candidates in:

  • Any field of law

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Develop a strong research proposal
  2. Identify potential supervisors
  3. Contact supervisors before applying

This early engagement can help applicants strengthen their applications and align research interests with faculty expertise.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must carefully review the scholarship requirements before applying.

Minimum Academic Requirements

Candidates must have:

  • A First-Class honours degree or a high 2.1 honours undergraduate degree in law (or equivalent)

In addition, applicants should also possess:

  • A Master’s degree in law or a related field

OR

  • Extensive and relevant professional experience demonstrating capacity for independent research and academic writing

Ideal Candidates for the Scholarship

The scholarship is best suited for applicants who:

  • Have strong academic records
  • Demonstrate research potential
  • Possess analytical and writing skills
  • Are passionate about legal scholarship
  • Intend to contribute to legal research and policy development

Applicants with interdisciplinary legal interests may also find this opportunity highly valuable.

Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion, and Equity

The School of Law at UCC strongly encourages applications from:

  • Under-represented groups in doctoral education
  • Individuals from diverse backgrounds
  • Candidates from fields where representation is limited

The institution has expressed a strong commitment to:

  • Diversity
  • Inclusion
  • Equity
  • Equal opportunity in higher education and research

This creates a welcoming and supportive environment for scholars from different countries, backgrounds, and experiences.

Why You Should Apply

Pursuing a fully funded PhD scholarship at an internationally respected university can significantly enhance your academic and professional future.

Benefits of this opportunity include:

  • Earning a doctorate from a leading Irish university
  • Conducting advanced legal research
  • Building international academic networks
  • Receiving generous financial support
  • Working with leading legal scholars
  • Gaining teaching and research experience
  • Strengthening future career opportunities in academia, policy, law, and international organisations

Application Process

Interested applicants are advised to:

  1. Prepare a strong research proposal
  2. Review all scholarship requirements carefully
  3. Contact potential supervisors
  4. Complete the official application process

Applicants should ensure that all required documents and information are submitted before the deadline.

Read the requirements and apply here: School of Law PhD Scholarship Requirements and Application Form 2026 

Application Deadline

Deadline: 19 June 2026 at 5:00 PM GMT

Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early.

Final Thoughts

The UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring legal scholars seeking fully funded doctoral study in Ireland.

With full tuition coverage, a generous annual stipend, expert supervision, and access to a vibrant academic community, the programme provides an excellent platform for conducting impactful legal research at an internationally respected institution.

For students who are passionate about law, policy, legal reform, and academic research, this scholarship could become a transformative step toward a successful international academic and professional career.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Fully Funded UCC School of Law PhD Scholarship 2026 in Ireland | €25,000 Annual Stipend for International and EU Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women 2026 in Ghana
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Investigative journalism remains one of the most powerful tools for promoting accountability, exposing corruption, combating disinformation, and strengthening democratic governance. However, women continue to be underrepresented in investigative and accountability journalism across many African countries, including Ghana. To bridge this gap and empower the next generation of female investigative journalists, the Media Foundation for West […]

The post Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women 2026 in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Investigative journalism remains one of the most powerful tools for promoting accountability, exposing corruption, combating disinformation, and strengthening democratic governance. However, women continue to be underrepresented in investigative and accountability journalism across many African countries, including Ghana.

To bridge this gap and empower the next generation of female investigative journalists, the Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA), with support from the European Union through International Media Support, has announced the 2026 Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women in Ghana.

This fully funded 12-month hybrid mentorship programme is designed to equip young Ghanaian women with practical investigative journalism skills, newsroom exposure, professional mentorship, and editorial support to become strong voices in accountability journalism.

The initiative forms part of the African Media Integrity and Resilience Programme being implemented by the MFWA.

About the Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme

The programme is targeted at female journalism graduates, early-career journalists, and young professionals who are passionate about governance reporting, investigative journalism, ethical media practice, and countering disinformation.

Selected participants will undergo a structured mentorship experience that combines residential training, virtual coaching, and newsroom attachment opportunities.

The programme aims to nurture women journalists who can contribute meaningfully to public accountability, transparency, and democratic development in Ghana.

Structure of the Programme

The mentorship programme will run from July 2026 to June 2027 and will follow a hybrid format.

1. Two Months Residential Training

Participants will begin with an intensive two-month in-person residential training programme.

Accommodation will be fully provided at a designated training location.

During this phase, fellows will receive hands-on training in:

  • Investigative journalism techniques
  • Governance and policy reporting
  • Leadership development
  • Mobile journalism
  • Fact-checking and verification
  • Multimedia storytelling
  • Digital content production
  • Women’s safety in journalism
  • Ethical and accountability reporting

2. Ten Months Virtual Mentorship

Following the residential component, participants will continue with ten months of virtual mentorship and professional coaching.

This stage will connect fellows with experienced female editors, investigative journalists, and governance experts who will guide participants throughout the programme.

The mentorship phase is designed to help fellows:

  • Strengthen investigative reporting skills
  • Develop editorial judgment
  • Build professional confidence
  • Improve storytelling techniques
  • Navigate challenges in journalism careers
  • Expand professional networks

3. Six-Month Newsroom Attachment

Within the mentorship period, participants will also complete a six-month newsroom attachment with The Fourth Estate, the investigative journalism project of the MFWA.

This practical newsroom experience will expose fellows to real investigative reporting projects and editorial processes.

Benefits of the Programme

Successful applicants will enjoy several professional and financial benefits throughout the mentorship programme.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Intensive investigative journalism training
  • Professional mentorship from experienced female editors
  • Coaching from governance experts
  • Financial support for investigative story production
  • Editorial support for published stories
  • Opportunity to produce at least five investigative stories
  • Six-month newsroom attachment with The Fourth Estate
  • Monthly stipend
  • Certificate of honour upon successful completion
  • Expanded professional journalism network
  • Enhanced digital and multimedia storytelling skills

Who Can Apply?

The programme is open to:

  • Female Ghanaian journalism graduates
  • Early-career journalists
  • Young professionals passionate about accountability journalism

Applicants must also be willing to fully participate in the programme from July 2026 to June 2027.

The organisers particularly encourage applications from candidates residing in:

  • Greater Accra Region
  • Eastern Region
  • Central Region
  • Ashanti Region
  • Bono East Region
  • Savannah Region
  • Northern Region
  • Upper West Region

Required Application Documents

Interested applicants must submit the following documents:

  1. A motivation letter (maximum 300 words) explaining why they should be considered for the programme
  2. Links to two published works:
    • News articles for journalists
    • Blog posts for other professionals
  3. Letter of consent from employer (required for staff journalists)
  4. Updated CV with two referees

How to Apply

Applicants should send all required documents via email to:

applications@mfwa.org

Email Subject Line:

“Application for Female IJ Mentorship”

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all requirements before submission to ensure their applications are complete.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women is:

May 27, 2026

Late applications may not be considered, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply early.

Why This Programme Matters

The underrepresentation of women in investigative journalism continues to limit diversity in newsrooms and public discourse. Programmes such as this mentorship initiative are critical in creating opportunities for women to build successful careers in investigative reporting and governance journalism.

By equipping young women with investigative skills, mentorship, and newsroom experience, the programme contributes to stronger media accountability systems and more inclusive journalism in Ghana.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for aspiring investigative journalists seeking practical exposure, professional guidance, and long-term career development in media and governance reporting.

For enquiries, applicants may contact Josephine Badu-Nyarko via:

  • Phone: 0547043404
  • Email: josephine@mfwa.org

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Media Foundation for West Africa Investigative Journalism Mentorship Programme for Women 2026 in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The United Nations Children’s Fund is now accepting applications for the Youth Engagement Internship under the Youth Partnerships and Programmes Secretariat of Generation Unlimited. This highly competitive international internship opportunity offers young professionals and graduate students the chance to contribute to global youth empowerment initiatives while working remotely with one of the world’s most respected […]

The post UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Children’s Fund is now accepting applications for the Youth Engagement Internship under the Youth Partnerships and Programmes Secretariat of Generation Unlimited. This highly competitive international internship opportunity offers young professionals and graduate students the chance to contribute to global youth empowerment initiatives while working remotely with one of the world’s most respected humanitarian organizations.

The internship is hosted under UNICEF’s Global Programme Division and is based in New York Headquarters (NYHQ), although the role will be carried out remotely on a full-time basis for a duration of 26 weeks.

UNICEF is recruiting two interns to support key areas within Generation Unlimited’s global youth engagement and innovation ecosystem. The opportunity is ideal for individuals passionate about youth development, entrepreneurship, international development, education, policy, and social impact.

Applications close on 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time).

About UNICEF

United Nations Children’s Fund operates in more than 190 countries and territories worldwide, working to protect children’s rights, improve access to education and healthcare, respond to emergencies, and support young people in reaching their full potential.

UNICEF’s mission centers on ensuring every child survives, thrives, learns, and is protected from harm. The organization works closely with governments, communities, civil society organizations, and international partners to create sustainable change for children and youth globally.

The organization is recognized internationally for its commitment to:

  • Child protection
  • Education
  • Health and nutrition
  • Youth empowerment
  • Emergency response
  • Social inclusion
  • Gender equality
  • Sustainable development

UNICEF also places strong emphasis on diversity, inclusion, innovation, and youth participation in global decision-making processes.

What is Generation Unlimited (GenU)?

Generation Unlimited is a global partnership initiative launched by UNICEF alongside public and private sector organizations.

The initiative focuses on expanding opportunities for young people aged 10–24 by helping them access:

  • Education
  • Skills training
  • Employment
  • Entrepreneurship opportunities
  • Digital inclusion
  • Social participation

Generation Unlimited aims to ensure that every young person can transition successfully from learning to earning by the year 2030.

The initiative particularly prioritizes vulnerable and marginalized youth groups, including:

  • Girls and young women
  • Youth living in poverty
  • Young people with disabilities
  • Migrants and displaced youth
  • Youth affected by conflicts
  • Youth impacted by climate disasters and emergencies

GenU brings together:

  • Governments
  • United Nations agencies
  • Private sector organizations
  • Academic institutions
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth-led groups
  • Investors
  • Development partners

The partnership works to identify, scale, and support innovative youth-centered solutions globally.

UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 Overview

Internship Details

Category Details
Organization UNICEF
Programme Generation Unlimited (GenU)
Internship Type Full-time Remote Internship
Duration 26 weeks
Number of Positions 2
Location Remote / New York HQ
Functional Areas Communication, Partnerships, Fundraising
Application Deadline 08 June 2026

Available Internship Positions

UNICEF is offering internship opportunities across two major hubs within Generation Unlimited.

1. Youth Engagement and Entrepreneurship Hub

This position focuses on youth participation, entrepreneurship programming, and youth-centered innovation initiatives.

Intern responsibilities include:

Youth Engagement

  • Supporting meaningful youth engagement strategies
  • Assisting the Young People’s Action Team globally and regionally
  • Supporting implementation of the Youth Centrality Strategy
  • Gathering feedback from marginalized youth groups
  • Helping integrate youth perspectives into programme design

Entrepreneurship Programming

  • Assisting entrepreneurship-focused initiatives
  • Supporting mentorship sessions and workshops
  • Helping young entrepreneurs build skills
  • Facilitating connections with strategic partners

Programme Support

  • Supporting youth-led programme implementation
  • Aligning projects with GenU strategic priorities
  • Assisting entrepreneurship and youth empowerment programmes

Advocacy and Outreach

  • Developing blogs and digital advocacy materials
  • Amplifying youth voices within GenU initiatives
  • Supporting youth participation in advocacy forums

2. Global Solutions Hub

This internship focuses on strategic planning, learning-to-earning initiatives, and knowledge-sharing activities.

Responsibilities include:

Global Solutions Portfolio

  • Supporting resources for UNICEF Country Offices
  • Assisting Learning-to-Earning programming initiatives
  • Supporting GenU programme deployment globally

Strategic Planning

  • Assisting the development of GenU’s 2026–2029 strategic plan
  • Supporting country consultations
  • Drafting technical notes and planning documents
  • Contributing to strategic reviews

Knowledge Sharing

  • Developing reports and blogs
  • Supporting peer-to-peer learning initiatives
  • Assisting webinars and thought leadership activities
  • Supporting online learning platforms and global youth networks

General Responsibilities of Interns

All interns will also support broader operational and programmatic activities, including:

Collaboration

  • Working closely with GenU programme teams
  • Coordinating with external partners
  • Supporting integrated programme delivery

Research and Analysis

  • Conducting research on youth development trends
  • Analyzing entrepreneurship and innovation issues
  • Supporting evidence-based strategy development

Administrative Support

  • Organizing meetings
  • Maintaining programme documentation
  • Supporting logistical coordination

The internship offers practical experience in:

  • International development
  • Programme management
  • Youth engagement
  • Policy support
  • Global partnerships
  • Advocacy
  • Research
  • Communication

Benefits of the UNICEF Internship

Selected interns will gain access to numerous professional development opportunities.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Hands-on international work experience
  • Opportunity to work with UNICEF and global partners
  • Exposure to international youth development initiatives
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Experience in global programme implementation
  • Flexible remote working arrangements
  • Leave entitlement
  • Reasonable accommodation support for persons with disabilities
  • Exposure to high-level global policy discussions
  • Skill development in research, advocacy, communication, and strategy

The internship also provides a strong foundation for careers in:

  • International development
  • Public policy
  • Youth advocacy
  • Humanitarian work
  • Social innovation
  • Entrepreneurship support
  • Global partnerships

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026, applicants must meet several academic and professional criteria.

Applicants Must:

  • Be enrolled in or have recently completed a graduate academic programme
  • Study fields such as:
    • International Relations
    • Political Science
    • Education
    • Business
    • Entrepreneurship
    • Monitoring and Evaluation
    • International Development
    • Other related disciplines

Applicants Should Also Have:

  • Interest or experience in youth development and engagement
  • Experience working with adolescents and youth programmes
  • Understanding of humanitarian or development contexts
  • Some volunteer or professional experience in youth-focused work
  • Strong writing and communication skills
  • Ability to prepare concept notes and pitch decks

Preferred Skills and Qualifications

UNICEF strongly prefers candidates with:

  • Knowledge of research and qualitative analysis
  • Experience with knowledge management
  • Data visualization skills
  • Experience participating in youth advisory bodies
  • Interest in GenU programme areas

Fluency in English is required.

Knowledge of additional UN languages is considered an advantage, including:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • French
  • Russian
  • Spanish

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents:

  1. Proof of university enrollment or recent graduation
  2. Official academic transcript
  3. Valid identification card
  4. Signed recommendation letter
  5. Proof of health insurance coverage for the internship duration

UNICEF’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

UNICEF emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout its recruitment process.

The organization welcomes applicants regardless of:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Gender identity
  • Disability
  • Nationality
  • Religion
  • Socio-economic background
  • Sexual orientation

UNICEF also maintains strict safeguarding and protection standards and conducts rigorous background checks on selected candidates.

Important Internship Notes

Applicants should note the following:

  • The internship does not guarantee future employment with UNICEF.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
  • Health insurance is mandatory.
  • UNICEF may withdraw internship offers if required documentation is not provided.
  • Selected candidates must comply with UNICEF safeguarding policies.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 08 June 2026 (Eastern Daylight Time)

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early before the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more and apply through the official UNICEF careers platform

Apply Here

For more information on this internship visit here 

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post UNICEF Youth Engagement Internship 2026 in New York (Remote): Fully Remote International Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The World Intellectual Property Organization Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland is now open for applications. This highly competitive United Nations internship offers an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates in law, ethics, public international law, compliance, and related fields to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations. […]

The post WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Intellectual Property Organization Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland is now open for applications. This highly competitive United Nations internship offers an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates in law, ethics, public international law, compliance, and related fields to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations.

Hosted at WIPO headquarters in Geneva, the internship provides successful candidates with practical exposure to ethics policy development, international civil service law, legal research, compliance frameworks, and ethics advisory work within the UN system. The internship is expected to begin on October 1, 2026, with an initial duration of six months and the possibility of renewal up to a maximum of twelve months.

The application deadline is June 19, 2026.

WIPO is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to promoting innovation and protecting intellectual property globally. Through this internship, selected interns will work directly within the Ethics Office under the supervision of the Chief Ethics Officer and contribute to maintaining integrity, ethical conduct, and accountability across the organization.

This opportunity is ideal for aspiring international lawyers, ethics professionals, compliance officers, policy researchers, and individuals interested in careers within the United Nations system.

About the WIPO Ethics Office Internship

The WIPO Ethics Office Internship Program is designed to help interns:

  • Complement their academic studies with practical international experience
  • Develop legal, analytical, and policy-related skills
  • Gain exposure to ethics and integrity systems within the United Nations
  • Understand international civil service law and governance
  • Build professional networks within international organizations

The internship is based entirely on-site in Geneva, Switzerland, and cannot be completed remotely.

Interns will work within the Ethics Office under the Sector of the Director General. The Ethics Office plays a crucial role in ensuring that all WIPO personnel uphold the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct according to:

  • The WIPO Convention
  • WIPO Staff Regulations and Rules
  • The WIPO Code of Ethics
  • Standards of Conduct for the International Civil Service

The office also provides confidential ethics advice, develops ethics-related policies, conducts awareness campaigns, and protects personnel from retaliation or reprisals.

Internship Location and Duration

Internship Location

  • Geneva, Switzerland

Expected Start Date

  • October 1, 2026

Internship Duration

  • Initial duration of 6 months
  • Renewable up to 12 months maximum

Working Arrangement

  • Full-time
  • 40 hours per week
  • On-site internship at WIPO headquarters

Financial Benefits and Internship Support

Selected interns will receive several benefits during the internship period.

Monthly Stipend

Interns will receive:

  • 2,170 Swiss francs per month

Additional Benefits

The internship package also includes:

  • Medical insurance
  • Annual leave
  • Sick leave benefits
  • Technical supervision and mentorship
  • Professional development within the UN system

This stipend is intended for full-time participation in the internship program.

What Interns Will Do

Interns will support the Ethics Office in legal, policy, communication, and research-related tasks.

Main Responsibilities Include

1. Ethics and Policy Research

  • Conduct research for new or revised ethics policies
  • Analyze ethics-related frameworks within the UN system
  • Support comparative legal and policy studies

2. Legal Research and Fact-Finding

  • Collect information and documentation related to ethics cases
  • Conduct jurisprudence and international civil service law research
  • Assist with legal analysis and policy interpretation

3. Ethics Advisory Support

  • Assist in preparing ethics-related guidance
  • Support the development of confidential advisory materials

4. Communication and Awareness Activities

  • Contribute to ethics outreach campaigns
  • Help prepare educational materials and presentations
  • Assist in awareness-raising initiatives

5. Documentation and Administrative Tasks

  • Draft memoranda, reports, and correspondence
  • Prepare PowerPoint presentations
  • Support updates to Ethics Office web pages and intranet content

6. Additional Tasks

  • Perform other related duties assigned by the Chief Ethics Officer

Eligibility Criteria

WIPO specifies that this internship falls under Category II internships.

Applicants must belong to one of the following categories:

Eligible Applicants Include

  1. Individuals pursuing their first advanced university degree who have completed coursework but not their final thesis
  2. Individuals who already hold one advanced degree and are pursuing another university degree
  3. Individuals who obtained their most recent advanced university degree less than two years before applying

Educational Requirements

Essential Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • An advanced degree in law (Master’s level preferred)

Preferred academic specializations include:

  • Public international law
  • Organizational ethics
  • Compliance
  • International civil service law

Desirable Qualification

  • Specialization in international civil service law

Preferred Experience

While not mandatory, WIPO values applicants with:

  • Previous experience in international organizations
  • Policy research experience
  • Experience developing communication materials
  • Familiarity with ethics or compliance systems

Language Requirements

Essential

  • Excellent written and spoken English

Desirable

  • Very good knowledge of French

Skills and Competencies Required

WIPO is seeking candidates with strong professional and interpersonal skills.

Key Competencies Include

  • Excellent analytical abilities
  • Strong legal drafting skills
  • Effective written and verbal communication
  • Teamwork and flexibility
  • Integrity and professionalism
  • Sound judgment
  • Confidentiality handling skills
  • Strong organizational capacity
  • Ability to work with sensitive information

Technical Skills

Applicants should also be proficient in:

  • Microsoft Office applications
  • Zoom
  • Digital collaboration tools
  • Online communication systems

Organizational Competencies

Interns are expected to demonstrate:

  • Effective communication
  • Integrity
  • Service orientation
  • Innovation and adaptability
  • Team spirit
  • Respect for diversity
  • Result-oriented performance
  • Continuous learning

Why This Internship Is Important

This WIPO internship is a major opportunity for individuals seeking careers in:

  • International law
  • Human rights
  • Ethics and compliance
  • UN agencies
  • International governance
  • Public policy
  • Diplomacy
  • Global institutions

Interns will gain first-hand exposure to how ethics systems function inside a major international organization and develop practical experience that can significantly strengthen future career prospects.

How to Apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026

Applicants must submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  • Motivation letter
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Submission Instructions

Applications should be sent by email to:

  • erecruit@wipo.int

Required Subject Line

  • Ethics Internship position 2026

Important Note

For LinkedIn Easy Apply submissions:

  • Combine both the CV and cover letter into one PDF file

Applications without both documents will not be considered.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 is:

June 19, 2026

Candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Applicants can learn more about the internship program on here

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY here

The post WIPO Ethics Office Internship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland | Paid United Nations Internship for Law and International Relations Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050. Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this […]

The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young writers, dreamers, storytellers, and changemakers from around the world are invited to participate in the Social Fiction Design Competition 2026, a global creative writing competition that challenges participants to imagine a better, fairer, and more sustainable world for young people in the year 2050.

Organized by Yunus Center and powered through YY Ventures, this international initiative encourages people everywhere to use the power of imagination and storytelling to envision solutions to today’s social and environmental challenges.

The competition is open globally with no age restrictions and no geographical limitations, making it one of the most inclusive international creative writing opportunities currently available.

Participants stand a chance to win a grand prize of USD 10,000 while contributing to conversations about the future of humanity, sustainability, innovation, and social transformation.

What is the Social Fiction Design Competition?

The Social Fiction Design Competition is an international writing competition focused on “Social Fiction” — a form of imaginative storytelling similar to science fiction, but centered on social innovation and human progress rather than futuristic technology alone.

The competition invites participants to imagine:

  • What the world could look like in 2050
  • How young people may live in the future
  • How societies can overcome inequality and injustice
  • What sustainable communities might become
  • How governments, businesses, and communities can work together for positive change

The goal is to inspire new ways of thinking about social innovation and encourage people to imagine the world they truly want to build.

According to Nobel Peace Laureate Muhammad Yunus:

“If we do not imagine, it will not be done. If we imagine, someday it will happen.”

This powerful idea forms the foundation of the competition.

What is Social Fiction?

Social Fiction is a storytelling approach that imagines a future society shaped by positive social transformation.

Unlike traditional science fiction, which often focuses heavily on futuristic machines or scientific advancement, Social Fiction focuses on:

  • Human-centered innovation
  • Social justice
  • Sustainability
  • Equality
  • Community transformation
  • Ethical development
  • Inclusive economies
  • Environmental healing

The concept encourages writers to creatively explore solutions to real-world problems and imagine societies where communities thrive.

The organizers believe that imagination can inspire real-world innovation and policy change.

Competition Theme: The Life of a Young Person in 2050

Participants are asked to imagine and describe:

“How do you wish to see the life of a young person in the year 2050?”

Writers are encouraged to take readers on a journey into a future world that is free from the social and environmental injustices affecting communities today.

The competition emphasizes optimism, innovation, and bold thinking.

Applicants should imagine a future where systems, institutions, and communities have evolved to create better opportunities and improved quality of life for younger generations.

Writing Requirements

Participants must submit an original Social Fiction piece between:

  • Minimum: 1,000 words
  • Maximum: 5,000 words

The writing should creatively explore a fictional world in 2050 while addressing one or more major social or environmental issues.

Suggested Focus Areas

Participants may choose to focus on one or several of the following areas:

  • Climate Change
  • Agriculture
  • Technology and Innovation
  • Micro-credit
  • Transport
  • Literature
  • Music
  • Waste Management
  • Circular Economy
  • Tourism
  • WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene)
  • Employment
  • Sports
  • Health and Well-being
  • Community Development
  • Sustainability
  • Social Equity

The organizers encourage participants to think broadly and creatively about how these sectors could evolve positively by 2050.

What Should Your Story Include?

The competition encourages writers to explain how different stakeholders contribute to building a better future.

Stories may include the role of:

  • Governments
  • Educational institutions
  • Corporate organizations
  • Local businesses
  • Community leaders
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Youth movements
  • Civil society organizations

Participants should demonstrate how collaboration and innovation help shape a more just and sustainable society.

Why This Competition Matters

The Social Fiction Design Competition is not just a writing contest. It is a platform for global imagination and social innovation.

At a time when the world faces challenges such as:

  • Climate change
  • Rising inequality
  • Unemployment
  • Environmental degradation
  • Mental health concerns
  • Economic instability
  • Social exclusion

The competition seeks bold visions of hope and possibility.

It encourages people to move beyond criticism and begin imagining actionable futures worth building.

Prize Opportunity: Win USD 10,000

One of the major attractions of the competition is the opportunity to win:

USD 10,000

The prize recognizes outstanding creativity, originality, optimism, and social imagination.

Beyond financial rewards, participants gain international exposure and become part of a global conversation on innovation and sustainable futures.

Who Can Participate?

One of the strongest features of the Social Fiction Design Competition is its accessibility.

There are:

  • No age restrictions
  • No geographical limitations

This means anyone from anywhere in the world can participate.

Students, writers, professionals, activists, researchers, entrepreneurs, and aspiring storytellers are all encouraged to apply.

Can You Apply More Than Once?

According to the competition FAQ:

  • Multiple applications are not permitted.

Applicants are encouraged to focus on submitting one strong and well-developed Social Fiction piece.

Is There an Application Fee?

The competition states that there is:

  • No application fee

Participation is completely free.

Can Teams Apply?

The FAQ section indicates that applicants should review the application guidelines regarding team participation.

Participants are encouraged to verify current submission rules through the official competition platform.

Previous Winners and Finalists

The competition has already attracted talented participants from different countries around the world.

Previous Winner

  • Amit Kumar Mishra – Nepal

Previous Finalists

  • Neha Sabu – India
  • Wan Faizal Mohd – Malaysia
  • Anne Julienne Barcelona – United States
  • Tyfur Rahman – Bangladesh

The diversity of past finalists demonstrates the truly international nature of the competition.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Social Fiction Design Competition can help writers and changemakers:

  • Showcase creativity globally
  • Build visibility as a thought leader
  • Contribute to social innovation conversations
  • Inspire real-world solutions
  • Develop storytelling and writing skills
  • Gain recognition for visionary ideas
  • Connect imagination with impact

For young innovators, students, activists, and creative thinkers, this is a unique opportunity to influence future thinking through storytelling.

How to Apply

Applicants are required to:

  1. Write an original Social Fiction piece
  2. Ensure the submission falls within 1,000–5,000 words
  3. Complete the online application form
  4. Submit the story through the official competition platform

For inquiries:

  • Email: info@yy.ventures
  • WhatsApp: +880 1648-397420

Apply Here 

For more information on this competition visit here 

Discover more opportunities on OFY here 

The post Social Fiction Design Competition 2026: Win USD 10,000 by Imagining a Better World for Young People in 2050 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BASE Fellows Fall 2026: Fully Remote AI Safety Fellowship Empowering Black Researchers and Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is now accepting applications from Black researchers, professionals, students, and practitioners interested in advancing their expertise in AI Safety, AI Governance, and AI Security. Designed as a fully remote and part-time fellowship, the programme offers a unique opportunity for members of the Black diaspora to gain hands-on research experience, […]

The post BASE Fellows Fall 2026: Fully Remote AI Safety Fellowship Empowering Black Researchers and Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is now accepting applications from Black researchers, professionals, students, and practitioners interested in advancing their expertise in AI Safety, AI Governance, and AI Security. Designed as a fully remote and part-time fellowship, the programme offers a unique opportunity for members of the Black diaspora to gain hands-on research experience, expert mentorship, and foundational training in some of the world’s most important and rapidly evolving fields in artificial intelligence.

Organised by Black in AI Safety and Ethics, the fellowship seeks to increase Black representation and leadership within the global AI ecosystem while ensuring that future AI systems are safe, equitable, accountable, and human-centered.

Applications for the BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme close on 16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth (AOE).

About the BASE Fellowship Fall 2026

The BASE Fellowship is a 13-week remote programme running from September 2026 through December 2026. The initiative is specifically designed to support and develop Black talent in critical AI-related fields that are shaping the future of technology and society.

The programme focuses on three major areas:

  1. AI Safety
  2. AI Governance
  3. AI Security

Through structured mentorship, collaborative learning, and practical research projects, fellows gain the knowledge and experience necessary to contribute meaningfully to the AI safety ecosystem and emerging governance discussions worldwide.

The fellowship aims to ensure that the Black diaspora remains actively involved in shaping the development and regulation of advanced AI systems rather than being excluded from these transformative conversations.

Mission of Black in AI Safety and Ethics (BASE)

Black in AI Safety and Ethics was established to create a connected and informed Black community within the fields of AI safety, governance, and security.

The organisation’s mission includes:

  • Increasing Black representation in AI leadership
  • Supporting Black researchers and practitioners
  • Promoting ethical and equitable AI systems
  • Building a global network of Black AI experts
  • Preventing the marginalisation of Black communities in the age of artificial intelligence
  • Ensuring fairness, accountability, and collective well-being in future AI systems

As artificial intelligence increasingly influences economies, governance, communication, employment, and public life, BASE seeks to guarantee that Black voices remain influential in shaping AI policy and technological development.

What Fellows Will Gain from the Programme

The BASE Fellowship offers substantial professional and academic benefits for selected participants.

1. Structured Expert Mentorship

Participants receive direct mentorship from experienced professionals and researchers working in AI safety, governance, and security.

This mentorship helps fellows:

  • Refine research skills
  • Develop technical and policy expertise
  • Navigate career opportunities in AI
  • Build long-term professional relationships

2. Hands-On Research Experience

Fellows work on real research projects aligned with their selected track and mentor expertise.

Participants gain practical experience in:

  • AI risk analysis
  • AI governance frameworks
  • AI safety methodologies
  • Policy development
  • Security considerations for advanced AI systems
  • Responsible AI deployment

3. Foundational Training in AI Safety Ecosystems

The fellowship equips fellows with critical foundational knowledge about the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

Training areas may include:

  • AI alignment
  • AI ethics
  • Frontier AI risks
  • AI policy and regulation
  • AI governance structures
  • Technical safety research
  • Human-centered AI systems

4. Networking and Community Building

Participants join a growing international network of Black researchers, professionals, and AI leaders.

This community provides opportunities for:

  • Future collaborations
  • Career advancement
  • Peer support
  • Research partnerships
  • Knowledge exchange

5. Leadership Development

The programme helps participants strengthen their leadership capacity in technical, interdisciplinary, and policy-focused AI spaces.

Fellows are encouraged to become future leaders capable of influencing the direction of AI development and governance globally.

Programme Structure and Timeline

The BASE Fellowship Fall 2026 is structured as a flexible part-time programme to accommodate participants’ academic and professional responsibilities.

Duration

  • 13 weeks

Programme Period

  • September 2026 to December 2026

Format

  • Fully remote
  • Part-time participation

Participants are expected to communicate their weekly availability and explain how they intend to balance the fellowship with existing commitments.

BASE Fellowship Selection Process

The fellowship selection process occurs in multiple stages to ensure that applicants are matched appropriately with mentors and research tracks.

Phase 1: Initial Application Review

Applicants first submit a comprehensive application including:

  • Resume/CV
  • Educational background
  • Work experience
  • Research interests
  • Motivation statement
  • Professional achievements
  • Track selection
  • Research and leadership experiences

Phase 2: Pre-Screening Assessment

Applicants who pass the initial review will receive:

  • A coding test for technical tracks, or
  • A work test for non-technical tracks

This stage evaluates the candidate’s readiness for the selected fellowship pathway.

Phase 3: Mentor Project Matching

Candidates who successfully pass the pre-screening stage will then apply to mentor-led projects aligned with their selected track.

Mentors review applicants and choose mentees based on compatibility, expertise, and research interests.

Phase 4: Final Offers

Final fellowship offers are issued directly by the BASE team after mentor selection is completed.

Eligibility Requirements

The fellowship is open to members of the Black/African diaspora interested in AI safety, governance, and security.

Applicants are expected to provide information regarding:

  • Educational background
  • Current employer or affiliation
  • Research interests
  • Technical or policy expertise
  • Leadership and project experiences
  • Time availability
  • Place of residence

Optional profile information may also include:

  • LinkedIn
  • Google Scholar
  • ORCID ID
  • GitHub
  • Personal website

Who Should Apply?

The BASE Fellowship is ideal for:

  • Black researchers in AI-related fields
  • Policy professionals interested in AI governance
  • Students exploring AI safety research
  • AI practitioners and technologists
  • Interdisciplinary researchers
  • Professionals seeking leadership opportunities in ethical AI

The fellowship welcomes applicants from technical, policy, and interdisciplinary backgrounds.

Why the BASE Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming every sector of society, from healthcare and education to governance, security, and employment. However, conversations around AI safety and governance often lack sufficient representation from Black communities globally.

The BASE Fellowship seeks to address this gap by creating pathways for Black professionals and researchers to participate actively in shaping the future of AI systems.

By supporting Black leadership in AI governance and safety, the programme contributes to building more equitable and inclusive technological futures.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme is:

16 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth (AOE)

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare all required documents before submission.

How to Apply

Applicants must complete the official online application form and submit all required materials before the deadline.

Official Fellowship Page: BASE Fellows Fall 2026

Application Portal: Apply for the BASE Fellowship 2026

Final Thoughts

The BASE Fellows Fall 2026 programme represents a major opportunity for Black professionals, researchers, and students seeking to shape the future of artificial intelligence in ethical, safe, and equitable ways.

With expert mentorship, hands-on research opportunities, and a growing global community, the fellowship helps participants position themselves at the forefront of AI safety, governance, and security discussions worldwide.

As AI technologies continue to influence humanity’s future, initiatives like the BASE Fellowship are essential for ensuring inclusive leadership and diverse representation within the global AI ecosystem.

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post BASE Fellows Fall 2026: Fully Remote AI Safety Fellowship Empowering Black Researchers and Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026: Free 14-Week Virtual AI Programme for Journalists, Editors, and Media Professionals Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The 2026 JournalismAI Skills Lab is now officially open for applications, offering media professionals across the globe an exceptional opportunity to deepen their expertise in artificial intelligence, generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and AI agents within journalism and newsroom environments. Organised by JournalismAI at London School of Economics and Political Science and supported by […]

The post JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026: Free 14-Week Virtual AI Programme for Journalists, Editors, and Media Professionals Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 JournalismAI Skills Lab is now officially open for applications, offering media professionals across the globe an exceptional opportunity to deepen their expertise in artificial intelligence, generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and AI agents within journalism and newsroom environments.

Organised by JournalismAI at London School of Economics and Political Science and supported by Google News Initiative, this fully virtual and completely free 14-week programme is designed for professionals working at the intersection of journalism and technology.

The Skills Lab goes far beyond basic prompt engineering. It provides practical, hands-on instruction that empowers participants to build AI-powered tools, prototypes, and proofs-of-concept that can directly contribute to innovation in newsrooms and media organisations worldwide.

Applications are currently open, and interested candidates must apply before 21 June 2026 at 11:59 PM GMT.

About the JournalismAI Skills Lab 2026

The JournalismAI Skills Lab is an intensive virtual training programme that equips journalism professionals with practical AI implementation skills. Unlike introductory AI courses, this programme focuses on helping participants actually build and deploy AI-powered solutions relevant to journalism, media workflows, audience engagement, newsroom innovation, and information verification.

Participants will learn how to use:

  • Large Language Models (LLMs)
  • Generative AI systems
  • AI agents
  • Python-based AI workflows
  • Journalism-focused AI applications
  • AI prototyping and proof-of-concept development

The programme’s primary objective is to help journalists and media professionals become active contributors to AI innovation inside newsrooms rather than passive users of AI technologies.

Programme Structure and Duration

The 2026 Skills Lab runs for 14 weeks in a live virtual format.

Weekly Commitment

Participants are expected to dedicate approximately:

  • 7 hours per week
  • Two live sessions weekly
  • Each session lasts two hours
  • Additional self-paced assignments and exercises

The programme combines:

  1. Live instructor-led classes
  2. Hands-on laboratory sessions
  3. Strategic product-thinking workshops
  4. Independent practical exercises
  5. Collaborative learning experiences

This blended approach ensures that participants gain both conceptual understanding and practical implementation skills.

Regional Cohorts for 2026

This year, the programme will be delivered through two regional cohorts:

Africa, Europe, and the Americas Cohort

  • 15 participants selected

Asia-Pacific and the Middle East Cohort

  • 10 participants selected

This structure enables more tailored collaboration, networking, and learning experiences across similar regional contexts.

What Participants Will Learn

The JournalismAI Skills Lab curriculum is designed to provide both technical and strategic AI knowledge for media professionals.

Core Learning Areas Include:

  • Building AI-powered newsroom tools
